content: Just as linguistic reflection in language learning requires learners to actively revisit their own writing to spot gaps in expression and align ideas with intended meaning, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow the structured metacognitive practice of high school language arts reflection curricula to improve their ability to spot inconsistent reasoning. This cross-pollination frames LLM self-reflection not as a novel engineering trick, but as a formalized skill that can be scaffolded through the same iterative, guided reflection routines used to teach human writing.
